title: Well-posedness of stochastic heat equation with distributional drift and skew stochastic heat equation
why_relevant: This is the core paper defining and proving well-posedness/approximation for stochastic heat equations with distributional drift, including b = δ0. Its approximation framework is directly needed for transferring irreducibility, Feller, or invariant-measure arguments from smooth drifts to the singular limit.

title: A skew stochastic heat equation
why_relevant: This paper studies a closely related stochastic heat equation with a singular skew/local-time drift and gives an explicit Gibbs-type invariant measure plus Dirichlet-form and approximation constructions. It is one of the most directly relevant sources for identifying possible invariant measures and proving uniqueness for the δ0-drift equation.

title: Analytically weak and mild solutions to stochastic heat equation with irregular drift
why_relevant: This recent paper clarifies weak and mild solution notions for stochastic heat equations with irregular/distributional drift. It is useful for checking that the Markov semigroup and approximation limits used in invariant-measure arguments are compatible with the ABLM solution concept.

title: Small ball probabilities and a support theorem for the stochastic heat equation
why_relevant: Small-ball and support estimates for the additive stochastic heat equation are a key route to irreducibility of the Markov semigroup. Such irreducibility, combined with strong/asymptotic Feller properties, is a standard proof of uniqueness of invariant measures.

title: Approximation and Support Theorem in Holder Norm for Parabolic Stochastic Partial Differential Equations
why_relevant: This classical support theorem for parabolic SPDEs in Hölder norms gives the control/Wong-Zakai machinery typically used to prove topological irreducibility. It is useful for showing that transition probabilities from different initial data have overlapping support.

title: A support theorem for parabolic stochastic PDEs with nondegenerate Hölder diffusion coefficients
why_relevant: This paper extends support theorems to rougher coefficients and bounded drifts. Its full-support estimates are relevant because the target equation is obtained as a singular drift limit and one may need robust support/irreducibility arguments.

title: The support of singular stochastic partial differential equations
why_relevant: This gives a modern support-theorem framework for singular SPDEs. Although aimed at regularity-structures settings, its support/controlled-equation methods are relevant to proving irreducibility for singular limits rather than only for smooth approximations.

title: Coupling and Invariant Measures for the Heat Equation with Noise
why_relevant: Mueller’s paper is an early direct coupling proof of uniqueness/mixing for stochastic heat equations with noise. Its coupling ideas are highly relevant if one tries to prove uniqueness of the invariant measure without relying solely on strong Feller theory.

title: Strong Feller Property and Irreducibility for Diffusions on Hilbert Spaces
why_relevant: This is a foundational result proving strong Feller and irreducibility for nondegenerate stochastic evolution equations, implying uniqueness of invariant measures. It is directly applicable to smooth drift approximations and provides the standard Hilbert-space strategy.

title: Irreducibility and strong Feller property for stochastic evolution equations in Banach spaces
why_relevant: The state space in the problem is C0([0,1]), a Banach space rather than just a Hilbert space. This paper gives Banach-space strong Feller/irreducibility criteria and applies them to stochastic nonlinear heat equations.

title: Ergodic behaviour of stochastic parabolic equations
why_relevant: This paper studies strong Feller irreducible Markov processes in Banach spaces and applies the results to stochastic parabolic equations. It contains exactly the type of uniqueness-of-invariant-measure criteria needed for the SPDE semigroup.

title: Invariant measures for nonlinear SPDE's: uniqueness and stability
why_relevant: This review collects uniqueness and stability criteria for invariant measures of nonlinear SPDEs, including strong Feller, irreducibility, recurrence, and convergence methods. It is useful for choosing the right abstract theorem for the present Markov semigroup.

title: Ergodic properties of recurrent solutions of stochastic evolution equations
why_relevant: This paper develops recurrence-based ergodic results for stochastic evolution equations. Such methods can help prove uniqueness of invariant measures when direct strong Feller estimates are hard to pass to a singular drift limit.

title: Asymptotic Properties of Stochastic Semilinear Equations by the Method of Lower Measures
why_relevant: The lower-measure method gives an alternative route to uniqueness and mixing of invariant measures for stochastic semilinear equations. This is useful if the δ0 drift obstructs a clean strong Feller proof.

title: On ergodicity of some Markov processes
why_relevant: This paper gives Polish-space criteria for existence and uniqueness of invariant measures using the e-property and lower bounds for transition probabilities. These criteria are potentially applicable to C0([0,1])-valued SPDEs where strong Feller may be difficult to establish.

title: On a Unique Ergodicity of Some Markov Processes
why_relevant: This paper refines uniqueness criteria based on asymptotic strong Feller/e-property ideas and applies them to nonlinear heat equations with noise. It is useful for proving at most one invariant measure from weaker continuity plus irreducibility assumptions.

title: Ergodicity of the 2D Navier-Stokes Equations with Degenerate Stochastic Forcing
why_relevant: This paper introduces the asymptotic strong Feller property and proves that it plus weak irreducibility yields uniqueness of the invariant measure. The abstract criterion is useful if the singular drift prevents proving ordinary strong Feller for Pt.

title: A Theory of Hypoellipticity and Unique Ergodicity for Semilinear Stochastic PDEs
why_relevant: This develops Malliavin/asymptotic-strong-Feller methods for semilinear parabolic SPDEs with additive noise. Its framework gives a possible route to smoothing estimates and uniqueness of invariant measures for the approximating equations and possibly their singular limit.

title: Uniqueness of the invariant measure for a stochastic PDE driven by degenerate noise
why_relevant: This paper proves invariant-measure uniqueness for a stochastic Ginzburg-Landau-type SPDE using smoothing and controllability ideas. It is relevant as a model proof of uniqueness for nonlinear stochastic heat-type equations.

title: Harnack Inequalities and Ergodicity of Stochastic Reaction-Diffusion Equation in Lp
why_relevant: Harnack inequalities are another standard route to strong Feller properties, irreducibility, and uniqueness of invariant measures for stochastic reaction-diffusion equations. The techniques may be adaptable to smooth δ0-approximations or limiting arguments.
